evals.prompt: Create a complete design token system for a fintech app. We have a primary blue, secondary green, and need semantic tokens for success, warning, error, and info states.
evals.expected_output: Produces a three-layer token architecture with primitive (raw hex values), semantic (purpose-mapped: success, warning, error, info), and component-level tokens, with CSS custom properties output.

assertions.text: Implements all three token layers: primitive (--color-blue-500), semantic (--color-status-success), and component (--button-primary-bg)
Generates CSS custom properties (--var) rather than hardcoded values in component styles
Includes all four semantic state tokens: success, warning, error, and info with light/dark variants
Provides a Tailwind theme extension config mapping tokens to utility class names
Documents the token hierarchy with a clear diagram or table showing primitive → semantic → component relationships

evals.prompt: Define the typography system for our design system. We use Inter for UI and Georgia for editorial content. Include all hierarchy levels.
evals.expected_output: Defines a complete typography scale with size, weight, line-height, and letter-spacing tokens for all hierarchy levels (display, h1–h4, body, caption, label), mapped as CSS variables.

assertions.text: Defines tokens for all expected hierarchy levels: display, h1, h2, h3, h4, body-lg, body, body-sm, caption, label
Maps Inter to UI elements and Georgia to editorial content with clear usage guidelines
Includes size, weight, line-height, and letter-spacing for each hierarchy level — not just font size
Uses a modular type scale (e.g., 1.25 or 1.333 ratio) rather than arbitrary size values
Provides CSS custom property declarations and Tailwind fontFamily/fontSize configuration

evals.prompt: Create component specifications for a button component. It needs primary, secondary, and ghost variants in default, hover, active, focus, disabled, and loading states.
evals.expected_output: Produces complete component specs for a button in 3 variants × 6 states, with token references for each visual property and accessibility annotations.

assertions.text: Covers all 6 states for each variant: default, hover, active, focus, disabled, and loading — 18 state definitions total
References design tokens (not hardcoded values) for background, text, border, and shadow in every state
Includes focus ring specification with WCAG 2.1 AA compliant contrast and offset values
Specifies disabled state with pointer-events: none, reduced opacity, and cursor: not-allowed
Provides size variants (sm, md, lg) with padding, font-size, and height tokens

evals.prompt: Build a spacing scale for our design system. We need consistent spacing from 2px to 96px used across padding, margin, and gap.
evals.expected_output: Generates a systematic spacing scale with named tokens on a consistent mathematical ratio, CSS custom properties, and usage guidance for padding, margin, and layout gap.

assertions.text: Uses a consistent mathematical progression (base-4 or base-8 scale) rather than arbitrary values
Provides named semantic tokens (--spacing-xs, --spacing-sm, etc.) in addition to numbered scale (--spacing-1, --spacing-2)
Covers the requested range from 2px to 96px with appropriate step granularity
Includes Tailwind spacing config to extend the default spacing utilities
Documents intended usage: which tokens are for padding, gap, section margin vs micro-spacing

evals.prompt: Generate a brand-compliant HTML presentation slide template using our design system tokens. It should work as a slide framework for our team.
evals.expected_output: Produces an HTML slide template that imports design system tokens as CSS variables and applies them to a complete slide layout framework with multiple slide types.

assertions.text: Imports all design system token CSS variables at the root level before any component styles
Applies tokens (not hardcoded values) for all visual properties: color, typography, spacing, and shadow
Includes multiple slide layout types: cover, content, two-column, and closing
Slide template is self-contained HTML that renders correctly in a browser without external dependencies
Includes a design system token reference comment block at the top of the file for maintainability
